sprint: World Builder — "My World Projects" lists Personal/Official/Growing, not just local
task: Closes the gap surfaced when an incognito window showed neither Personal nor Official Themes: 'my world projects should list all my personal, official and growing projects... without any local storage.' Confirmed via AskUserQuestion (not assumed): Official-only entries stay permanently read-only (a small standalone info modal, matching View Mode's existing rule); Personal-only entries restore as a fully editable Workspace when a Builder Project Cloud Backup exists for them (js/services/projectSync.js gained list()), cross-referenced by World Id -- the REAL original Project JSON is restored, never a lossy reconstruction from the compiled Theme, which has no Scene/Place/Experience data to reverse. worldBuilderApp.js's existing _annotateProjectBadges now also renders a card for every Repository row with no matching local Project, and renderMyWorlds()'s empty-state early-return was removed so a genuinely empty local list still shows Repository content. A disclosed structural limit carries over from the anonymous-auth model: a genuinely different browser/incognito profile gets its own new auth.uid() and correctly cannot see another session's Personal rows -- this restores what the CURRENT session's own identity already owns, not cross-device sync. Verified via Playwright using a real Create-New-World-generated fixture (not hand-rolled) for the restore path; goldenBuild.js's 30-assertion suite and every prior Duplicate/Quota/Thumbnail/Holders/Storage-Meter regression script pass unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
